message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should reject entire claim when damage entries exceed insured count for that type"
Expected behavior: processClaim should throw an error when damage entries of a given type exceed the number of that type in the policy
Current error: expected [Function] to throw an error (processClaim only processes damages[0] and doesn't validate count)
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Context:
- Before processing, count damage entries per type
- Compare to count of policy items per type
- If any type has more damage entries than insured items, throw Error
- The whole claim is rejected (no partial processing)

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: Let me read the current state of the files.

Now I understand the failing test. The test has one sword in the policy but two sword damage entries. The current `processClaim` only processes `damages[0]` and doesn't validate count. I need to add validation that counts damage entries per type and compares to the count of policy items per type.

The minimal implementation: before processing, count damage entries by type, count policy items by type, and throw if any type has more damage entries than insured items.

content.text:
All 26 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added pre-processing validation in `processClaim` that counts damage entries per type and policy items per type, then throws an error if any damage type has more entries than insured items of that type.
**Result**: All 26 tests now pass
**Approach**: Minimal - only adds the count comparison needed to satisfy the new test. The existing single-damage processing logic is untouched; the new validation runs first and throws before any processing occurs when the count is exceeded.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.

Relevant file: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_07-09-01_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6/src/claim-office.ts`
